post_rendered:
Mafia Game 226!
Blotted

  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification, but I will elect to answer only if I deem such clarification legitimate.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Scum chat will be via QuickTopic.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, Zwjs text, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.

  • Voting/Hanging
    • Post votes in the format 
      Vote: <insert player here>
      . Usernames must be typed exactly.
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I make the official end of the game post.
    • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: Sleep
      .
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, in fact you do not have the ability to do so.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is hanged. If there is a tie the player who received the votes first is hanged. If Sleep is part of the tie, No Hang occurs regardless.
    • If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, Night immediately begins and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post. The person hanged (if any) is officially eliminated.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be seconds, where is the number of players remaining. The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion.
    • All posted times will be based on Hawaii Standard Time.
    • The deadline will occur the very second the day has ended.
    • There will be two night deadlines, one will be 24 hours after night begins for all town actions, and the other will be 48 hours after night begins for all mafia actions.
    • When a night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from a player due at that time, the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night. In the case of the mafia's mandatory nightkill, a townie will be selected at random.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, they will receive an in-game prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, or a player has received too many PM prods, they will be replaced, unless they post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, they must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod or co-mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • I (Osmium_) am the moderator of Mafia Game 226. He (cherryolive) is the comod of Mafia Game 226.
    • This viridian (#517861) is my mod color. This blue (#3D85C6) is cherryolive's mod color.
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The co-mod has the second to last say.

Setup
Setup is Mostly-Open. (The only hidden information is the nature of three special prizes.)

Town
- 7 Vanilla Townie
Osmium_ wrote:
"The absence of power can be another form of power." Do confirm by using the private line provided to tell what you understand of this quote.

- 1 Motion-Detector
Osmium_ wrote:
Consider yourself one of those partially informed. Every night you may communicate to me the name of any still competing player including yourself and I will tell you if any actions were performed by or on this person, but not what they were, or who else was involved. Submissions will be due 24 hours after day end. Do confirm by using the private line provided to tell what you believe is the most effective use of such ability.

- 1 Town Mailman
Osmium_ wrote:
Consider yourself an informant but not an informed. Every night you may communicate to me a short message that I will send to a living player of your choice that is not yourself. Messages are 50 characters maximum, all text. It will be made clear to the receiver that the message is from another person and are not GM-confirmed. Submissions will be due 24 hours after day end. Do confirm by using the private line provided to tell what you believe is the most effective use of such ability.

- 1 Town Oracle
Osmium_ wrote:
Consider yourself as having traded power for the target on your back. Every night you may communicate to me a partition of the set of still competing players including yourself into 2 groups A and B, with a maximum difference of one in size. I will tell you which group has more scum and if they have equal numbers then one will be chosen at random. Submissions will be due 24 hours after day end and once submitted, results (including groupings) are reported back to the scumteam. Your results are reported back at day start. Do confirm by using the private line provided to tell the name of the player you believe would use this role most effectively.


Scum
- 1 Mafia Goon
Osmium_ wrote:
Along with #7 and #11 you collectively hold the power of collusion with the private triangle provided below. As a collective you have my permission to select a player to eliminate each night. However one of you must be chosen to carry out this action. Do confirm by using the private line provided to tell what your favorite flavor of ice cream is.

- 1 Mafia Roleblocker
Osmium_ wrote:
Along with #3 and #7 you collectively hold the power of collusion with the private triangle provided below. As a collective you have my permission to select a player to eliminate each night. However one of you must be chosen to carry out this action. In addition, every night you may send me the name of any still competing player including yourself and they will be unable to perform their abilities that night. Do confirm by using the private line provided to tell what your favorite flavor of ice cream is.

- 1 One-Shot Mafia Strongman
Osmium_ wrote:
Along with #3 and #11 you collectively hold the power of collusion with the private triangle provided below. As a collective you have my permission to select a player to eliminate each night. However one of you must be chosen to carry out this action. In addition, if you are the one chosen to do so, you may at most once per game perform an ability-proof elimination. Do confirm by using the private line provided to tell what you think this means.


Blotto! Tourney
On each day that they are scheduled, there will be a Blotto minigame, played in a instantaneous single-elimination tournament format. 2nd and 3rd places will receive a shared Quicktopic as their prize, while 1st place will receive a predetermined secret prize. More details to be announced on the days of.


In Contention (6)
  1. Mathletesv
  2. falliinqsnow-
  3. VKU427
  4. snoozingnewt
  5. awesomeming327.
  6. Socoobo


Eliminated (7)
  1. D1: violetmx12 - Vanilla Townie
  2. N1: mollyq - Mailman
  3. D2: benjaminsz - One-Shot Mafia Strongman
  4. N2: Scipio1 - Vanilla Townie
  5. D3: HistoricFoodie - Vanilla Townie
  6. N3: NDMath - Oracle
  7. D4: EulerNTM - Mafia Goon

Day 1 Practice Round 1 Blotto Tournament Begins

Day 1 Practice Round 1 Blotto Tournament Results

Day 1 Practice Round 2 Blotto Tournament Results & Day 1 Blotto Tournament Begins

Night 1 Ends & Day 2 Begins

Day 2 Practice Round 1 Blotto Tournament Results

Day 2 Snap Blotto Tournament Results
Day 2 Blotto Tournament Begins

Day 3 Activity Check Blotto Results


You thought you were smart. Maybe not the best in history, for how could you gauge the skills of past masters, but certainly one of the best in the world at present. And if only to confirm your ego, you received an invitation to the exclusive International Blotto Grandmasters Tournament. Blotto: A true test of multi-faceted intelligence. It was the perfect game, and you knew that this would be one of your most exciting endeavors yet. The organizer had clearly done their research in finding you. This was more than a mere tournament.

You would win, you told yourself as you surveyed the competition. As expected, all were serious and evidently everyone had the same idea you had. No one spoke as your group collectively waited for the appearance of the host. Your patience was soon awarded by the sound of static. A doubtlessly expensive sound system carried over a person's words from everywhere at once.

"Greetings. You may call me Osmium. Infer what you will from that. This is a single-elimination blotto tournament. But you already knew that, and I suppose you all already knew that this is a game. A game within a game. Let me briefly explain the rules. I am your gamemaster. In front of you is my assistant. You may refer to him as cherryolive."

In the front of the lobby where none had stood before, stood a man in butler attire, ramrod straight, and patiently still - following the stereotype to a tee. He bowed now that everyone's eyes were on him.

"For the duration of this game, you are to remain in these premises reserved by me from the Blue Grotto establishment. Rest assured that you will be well-accommodated. Each day represents a new round of the tournament. Please, feel free to mingle and make the acquaintances of your fellow competitors. I assume you know very well how much this might be useful to you. At noon, there will be a single-elimination blotto tournament as advertised. There are incentives for your performance. Of course, you are free to discuss the events of this "tournament inside a tournament" once it has finished. At the end of the day, a democratic election will by held by which you may collectively choose up to one person to eliminate. During night, up to one additional competitor will be eliminated. Do you all understand?"

Of course. Oh this was good. While you took some pity on those who would be unfortunately eliminated, it did not matter; you would survive and win the real game. 13. The number of contestants had been bugging you for a while, but now, it perfectly aligned with the revealed true purpose. How clever. And by the looks on everyone's faces, they understood too. Heh. You would really love to have a battle of wits with the gamemaster after the dust settled. How had they managed to gather up a group so perceptive?

"Located on your electronic IDs is a SmartTagTM which you may scan using your phones to receive further individualized rules and instructions. Do not lose it. I will not bore you by discussing their contents; you all already know how to play Blotto and many other things. Instead, keep a watchful eye out for what is happening behind the scenes. And let this be your first and final warning: Be smart. Read hard. Think hard. It may just be crucial to your enjoyment."

As suddenly as it came, the voice was gone. You mentally arched your eyebrows. Not even a good luck wish? It was only proper manners, but you supposed it might be better this way, what with the current audience and circumstances. You straightened a fake cordial smile and began to exit with the other competitors, eager to begin the small talk, as you plotted ways of ensuring your success. Oh how fun it would be, to triumph over worthy opponents!


Day 4+ε

post_rendered: I'm voting HF to see how they react. (Is this a site meta thing?). Like, I find HF's comment really extremely odd, whether they're scum or not, because I can't imagine he hasn't played enough games to realize that meta analysis, especially on a new player, is not always super accurate. Because I find it odd, I want to get a better read on him. To do that, I vote, which puts more pressure on him than just calling him out would have. Sometimes causes teammates to panic and try to intervene, if I happen to have actually voted a wolf. Hmmm I guess this might be less common here since a single vote means less--games on Sanderson Elimination have a different hanging system, where the person with the most votes at the end of the Day is hanged even if the majority of people didn't vote, so putting a single vote on someone during the first Day can end with them dead pretty easily.

post_rendered:
mollyq wrote:
I'm voting HF to see how they react. (Is this a site meta thing?). Like, I find HF's comment really extremely odd, whether they're scum or not, because I can't imagine he hasn't played enough games to realize that meta analysis, especially on a new player, is not always super accurate. Because I find it odd, I want to get a better read on him. To do that, I vote, which puts more pressure on him than just calling him out would have. Sometimes causes teammates to panic and try to intervene, if I happen to have actually voted a wolf. Hmmm I guess this might be less common here since a single vote means less--games on Sanderson Elimination have a different hanging system, where the person with the most votes at the end of the Day is hanged even if the majority of people didn't vote, so putting a single vote on someone during the first Day can end with them dead pretty easily.

Voting people to see how they react is part of a subgroup of things known as RT's (Reaction tests) Reaction tests are what the name implies, but they don't have to be votes. Overusing RTs is part of FPS, which is called Fancy Play Syndrome, which is also what the name implies.

"Sometimes causes teammates to panic and try to intervene, if I happen to have actually voted a wolf. Hmmm I guess this might be less common here since a single vote means less-"
I agree, that's usually what happens. Wolf teammates try to ignore the vote, or may even bandwagon on their partner, as long as they know their scumpartner isn't in danger of being hanged.

For the sake of creating conversation that is not about HF, here are my reads on everyone else:

MSV: nullish, not really able to get anything from a lower number of posts that aren't highly indicative
faliinq: At first glance is weirdly self-conscious but also non-contributive/lurking, but that could just be the effect of it being IRL D1/RVS so I'm putting this at null for now.
quinn: I have no clue how good she is because I don't know her site meta/experience, but she appears towny at first glance, and I don't know whether she has the skill to fake the posts she's been making or not because I feel like they come from a pretty pure place. post_rendered:
Announcement:

It's Blotto! time. The first practice round starts now for all intents and purposes. There is no deadline for submission for practice rounds, but just note that if any Blotto event is still running by EoD, the hang will be randomized among all living players, so please don't dawdle. Results will be computed from the moment of the last submission from all players although you may change your submission any time before then. You may discuss any and all aspects of the event in thread. There is an incentive for doing well on both practices.

Format: Practice rounds will be held publicly: this means that Blotto submissions through PM will be exempt from the confidentiality rule so that placings and scores can be announced. Practice events are all round-robin tournaments; each pair of players will face off with their privately submitted entries and gain a point for each win. For D1 Practice Round 1, ties will be allowed and worth 0.5 points each. This will not be the case for D1 Practice Round 2, in which a simple tie-breaking mechanism will be used.

Still don't know how to play Blotto? Its simple. All Day 1 Blotto rounds will be using vanilla [100, 10]. Everyone will submit a list of 10 nonnegative integers that sum to 100. Please make sure they sum to at most 100 and don't make me mad. For example, person A submits [1,3,5,7,10,10,13,15,17,19] and person B may submit [10,10,10,10,10,10,10,10,10,10]. When person A and person B face off, each person wins points for every space where they have a greater number. In this case, A wins slots 7-10 and B wins slots 1-4, for 4 points each. Therefore the match is a draw, and each of A and B win 0.5 points to contribute to their overall standing in the tournament.

I repeat, you may discuss strategy in thread, and there is no real deadline for submission. Feel free to ask questions.
post_canonical:
[b]Announcement:[/b]

It's Blotto! time. The first practice round starts now for all intents and purposes. There is no deadline for submission for practice rounds, but just note that if any Blotto event is still running by EoD, the hang will be randomized among all living players, so please don't dawdle. Results will be computed from the moment of the last submission from all players although you may change your submission any time before then. You may discuss any and all aspects of the event in thread. [i]There is an incentive for doing well on both practices.[/i]

Format: Practice rounds will be held publicly: this means that Blotto submissions through PM will be exempt from the confidentiality rule so that placings and scores can be announced. Practice events are all round-robin tournaments; each pair of players will face off with their privately submitted entries and gain a point for each win. For D1 Practice Round 1, ties will be allowed and worth 0.5 points each. This will not be the case for D1 Practice Round 2, in which a simple tie-breaking mechanism will be used.

Still don't know how to play Blotto? Its simple. All Day 1 Blotto rounds will be using vanilla [100, 10]. Everyone will submit a list of 10 nonnegative integers that sum to 100. Please make sure they sum to at most 100 and don't make me mad. For example, person A submits [1,3,5,7,10,10,13,15,17,19] and person B may submit [10,10,10,10,10,10,10,10,10,10]. When person A and person B face off, each person wins points for every space where they have a greater number. In this case, A wins slots 7-10 and B wins slots 1-4, for 4 points each. Therefore the match is a draw, and each of A and B win 0.5 points to contribute to their overall standing in the tournament.

I repeat, you may discuss strategy in thread, and there is no real deadline for submission. Feel free to ask questions.

Conclusions (disclaimer: these are for the first hundred posts only and are subject to change. “Slight town” and “slight scum” are the strongest reads I’m giving here):
snow:ll three posts that snow had made by post 100 were NAI as far as I could tell. I’m going to put that as slight scum now that I think about it--this is mostly based off of skimming the posts of hers that snewt quoted, which do seem more solve-y than her posts this game.

Scipio I read as null-town. He’s been questioning people for unclear statements, and the things he’s said have generally made sense.

Socoobo: I also read everything that Socoobo said in the first 100 posts as NAI. I’m leaving him as true null, though, because I have no reference point for him unlike snow and also I think in his case that changes later? I still fully intend to go through the rest of the thread with a fine-toothed comb.

Mathlete I’m reading null-town or slight town for essentially the same reasons as Scipio. He seems to be genuinely trying to get reads, and questioning others for their reads if they aren’t explained well--trying to get reasoning from others, etc. Also just feels generally towny.

HF: okay, so first of all, this is entirely before the VT claim and I’m ignoring that entirely in my read of him. On a careful reread, I actually find him quite town. One thing that seems to have caused a lot of confusion around HF is that he doesn’t quote things, so for example his question about why he was being read scum was taken as general when really it was specifically directed at awesomeming, the only person who had scumread him. Further, looking at his posts--the thing about snow and her meta actually reads quite towny to me now: he originally concluded that her meta was similar to in 222, and reconsidered after snewt cited some specific differences. You can actually see a logical progression in his thinking, which is not something that I’d think an inexperienced scum player would be able to fake. So, slight town on HF.

snoozingnewt surprisingly had more AI posts than I’d thought I’d find, all of them town-ish. He had a lot of NAI posts, of course, but in between them he went back and did a genuine reread of 222, quoted some of snow’s posts from there, and pointed out how her meta was different, which seems to have been what changed HF’s mind. That is, snewt has posts that are actually effort into solving. So, slight town on him as well.

ming... I… okay, so all of ming’s posts were NAI or null town to me. The null town ones were her responses to Mathlete’s questions about why she read me so town. She immediately pointed to a specific post of mine, saying that she couldn’t see it coming from scum. While this is not a great argument when used on me (on my home site that kind of post is pretty common from scum who want to look town) it makes sense for this forum. I’m reading her as null town because she had an actual, specific, concrete reason for her read.

Euler had like two posts that read very slightly town to me, but overall I read him completely null. The only thing I got from my reread--which was something I’d missed before--is that Euler town-read HF even before there was pressure on HF for his snow-meta-thing. Again, natural progression of thoughts, etc. this makes Euler’s defense of HF seem at least more reasonable to me. Also, if Euler is scum then HF is definitely town, because I’m pretty sure that Euler is experienced enough not to link himself to a teammate in a situation where it’s not necessary. But, still, reading Euler null.

To the point that I’ve analyzed, no one else had posted.

Conclusions (disclaimer: these are for the [i]first hundred posts only[/i] and are subject to change. “Slight town” and “slight scum” are the strongest reads I’m giving here): 
[b]snow:[/b]ll three posts that snow had made by post 100 were NAI as far as I could tell. I’m going to put that as slight scum now that I think about it--this is mostly based off of skimming the posts of hers that snewt quoted, which do seem more solve-y than her posts this game.

[b]Scipio[/b] I read as null-town. He’s been questioning people for unclear statements, and the things he’s said have generally made sense. 

[b]Socoobo:[/b] I also read everything that Socoobo said in the first 100 posts as NAI. I’m leaving him as true null, though, because I have no reference point for him unlike snow and also I think in his case that changes later? I still fully intend to go through the rest of the thread with a fine-toothed comb.

[b]Mathlete[/b] I’m reading null-town or slight town for essentially the same reasons as Scipio. He seems to be genuinely trying to get reads, and questioning others for their reads if they aren’t explained well--trying to get reasoning from others, etc. Also just feels generally towny.

[b]HF:[/b] okay, so first of all, this is entirely before the VT claim and I’m ignoring that entirely in my read of him. On a careful reread, I actually find him quite town. One thing that seems to have caused a lot of confusion around HF is that he doesn’t quote things, so for example his question about why he was being read scum was taken as general when really it was specifically directed at awesomeming, the only person who had scumread him. Further, looking at his posts--the thing about snow and her meta actually reads quite towny to me now: he originally concluded that her meta was similar to in 222, and reconsidered after snewt cited some specific differences. You can actually see a logical progression in his thinking, which is not something that I’d think an inexperienced scum player would be able to fake. So, slight town on HF.

[b]snoozingnewt[/b] surprisingly had more AI posts than I’d thought I’d find, all of them town-ish. He had a lot of NAI posts, of course, but in between them he went back and did a genuine reread of 222, quoted some of snow’s posts from there, and pointed out how her meta was different, which seems to have been what changed HF’s mind. That is, snewt has posts that are actually effort into solving. So, slight town on him as well.

[b]ming[/b]... I… okay, so all of ming’s posts were NAI or null town to me. The null town ones were her responses to Mathlete’s questions about why she read me so town. She immediately pointed to a specific post of mine, saying that she couldn’t see it coming from scum. While this is not a great argument when used on me (on my home site that kind of post is pretty common from scum who want to look town) it makes sense for this forum. I’m reading her as null town because she had an actual, specific, concrete reason for her read.

[b]Euler[/b] had like two posts that read very slightly town to me, but overall I read him completely null. The only thing I got from my reread--which was something I’d missed before--is that Euler town-read HF even before there was pressure on HF for his snow-meta-thing. Again, natural progression of thoughts, etc. this makes Euler’s defense of HF seem at least more reasonable to me. Also, if Euler is scum then HF is definitely town, because I’m pretty sure that Euler is experienced enough not to link himself to a teammate in a situation where it’s not necessary. But, still, reading Euler null.

To the point that I’ve analyzed, no one else had posted.

post_rendered:
Announcement:

It's Blotto! time. The second practice round starts now for all intents and purposes. There is no deadline for submission for practice rounds, but just note that if any Blotto event is still running by the standard EoD deadline, an extra player will be chosen and eliminated at random, so please don't dawdle. Results will be computed from the moment of the last submission from all players although you may change your submission any time before then. You may discuss any and all aspects of the event in thread. There is an incentive for doing well on this practice.

Format: Practice rounds will be held publicly: this means that Blotto submissions through PM will be exempt from the confidentiality rule so that placings and scores can be announced. Practice events are all round-robin tournaments; each pair of players will face off with their privately submitted entries and gain a point for each win. The change for Practice Round 2 is that ties will no longer be allowed. If two players manage to win the same number of slots, then the player who won the leftmost non-tied slot wins. If all slots are tied, then both players lose. The same policy will hold true in the real Day 1 tournament.

I repeat, you may discuss strategy in thread, and there is no real deadline for submission. Feel free to ask questions.

So no discussion is happening right now and I think that's a HUGE waste. We've been granted what is effectively an ultra-long twilight and I think it's best that we use it.

Unfortunately we don't get much out of violet's flip bc of how much nothing she did - so I guess it's time to talk about other things instead!

It's not worth discussing hangs until night is over of course, but we can still post updated reads and stuff. So here are mine:

MSV - Tilt town. He's doing the bare minimum for me to consider him contributive, which would be pretty scummy if he were a better player but as it is just makes me think he's a standard unconfident newbtown. However, I haven't got a strong read at all here.
Me - conftown FMPOV obviously
Faliinq - Lean town. She has responded to a bad attack with annoyance, but not OMGUS. That almost always indicates town - only really good scum players will do it as scum, as less experienced players tend to be worried that if they don't put up a super strong defense they'll get wagoned on. I have not previously observed faliinq to have the level of confidence required to do this as scum. Besides, she's been solidly contributive and on meta (her 4 RVS posts notwithstanding).
Quinn - Null. Honestly I always nullread offsite players at first. If we both make it to LyLo I'll metadive her site, but otherwise I probably won't have time to go to the effort to get a good read on her.
Scipio - Tilt town. Scipio is actually experienced enough to be doing the scum semi-lurking thing, but there's no reason for me to scumread him other than paranoia, and he's been helpful and said things scum doesn't need to say a lot.
HF - Hard lean town. This has been explained enough times already.
VKU - Null. He's playing like Madelyn but even lurkier, and I have no clue what that means since he isn't Madelyn.
Snoozing - Either lean scum or lean town, but defo not null. I know that sounds paradoxical, but I have strong opinions on snoozing, they just lead in both directions. I really don't like the way he's playing, but it also makes sense for town snoozing to play that way.
Ming - Lean town. Ming is playing like the classic newbtown - a mixture of sheeping, using bad logic in an earnest attempt to contribute, and posting quite a fair bit.
SCB - Lean scum. He's made a lot of attacks that are just silly, purposefully strawmanned a lot, and generally done lots of things that he should know are bad - should be obvious to everyone are bad - but he's still doing them. I'll ISO him later once we have to decide a D2 hang, but this is just my opinion for now.
Euler - Null to tilt scum. A few questionable things, but mostly just generic midtier experienced newbie play, nothing leaning to heavily one direction or another.
Hannah slot - Lean town, but this will obviously be heavily influenced by the replacement. Hannah played a lot like ming, and I TR her for the same reasons, but reading a slot in the middle of replacement is kind of dumb, so make of that what you will.

 VKU - Null. He's playing like Madelyn but even lurkier, and I have no clue what that means since he isn't Madelyn.

Ming - Lean town. Ming is playing like the classic newbtown - a mixture of sheeping, using bad logic in an earnest attempt to contribute, and posting quite a fair bit.
SCB - Lean scum. Hannah slot - Lean town, but this will obviously be heavily influenced by the replacement. Hannah played a lot like ming, and I TR her for the same reasons, but reading a slot in the middle of replacement is kind of dumb, so make of that what you will.

post_rendered:
Today had been ... a day to remember. The rules and rule changes were simply excessive, and whether or not you were the type of person who liked that kind of thing, this combined with Osmium's overzealous attitude made the morning a fraught and contentious adjustment period. More than unpleasant. But once the games started, we all had a pleasant distraction to focus on. No more gamemaster insults, just some door banging on the room's of some competitors who liked to take their sweet time with things.

Not that that was to be taken lightly. Before noon had even been reached, we had a particularly infuriating one, nicknamed Violet, one vote away from elimination. Upon coming out of their closet, they quickly voted themselves. A suicide. It seems that they really hadn't been interested in playing. I didn't say anything publicly about it, but a closer inspection revealed that Violet had in fact felt overwhelmed and unable to make progress. That they were an uninformed was all but obvious. Still, the day went on, the Violet incident was given little attention, and the rest of the day was successfully devoted to more Blotto.

The next day won't be so easy, will it?

An unnatural smile creeps up on the speaker's face. It's getting late, and many have gathered to enjoy the rumored Hawaian sunset on the first floor Balcony West #2. 41 floors above them, a pair was also enjoying the fading rays of light.

"How was it?"
"Wonderfully flavored, but the texture could use some improvement."
"Is that a clever metaphor?"
"You think too highly of me. No."

"Frustrating but interesting."
"I see."
"Night begins and many things lurk in the shadows. How will they respond? ... By bungling it of course."
"May I take the other side of this bet?"
"Certainly. Just don't give them ideas. Surprises need to stay surprises. Because at the end of it all, what matters most was that it was sufficiently interesting."

The push notification system rings an alert to 12 Smart IDsTM.

It is now Night 1. Secret activities should be carried out either by midnight or daybreak. Voting is illegal, so enjoy this period of rest. Night 1 has been shortened by 154 minutes.

post_rendered: Yeah IK a lot of the things in any given ISO are going to be more NAI than you want them to be. That's why you come up with every reason possible, to increase the odds that at least one of them is correct. If there aren't enough reasons to SR or TR in your ISO, then odds are it's NAI. But if there are lots of reasons, then odds are pretty good that the ISO overall is AI.

post_rendered:
Announcement:

It's Blotto! time. The third practice round starts now for all intents and purposes. There is no deadline for submission for practice rounds, but just note that if any Blotto event is still running by EoD, the hang will be randomized among all living players, so please don't dawdle. Results will be computed from the moment of the last submission from all players although you may change your submission any time before then. You may discuss any and all aspects of the event in thread. There is an incentive for doing well on practices in general and the incentive has been recalibrated to be stronger than it was last time.

Format: Practice rounds will be held publicly: this means that Blotto submissions through PM will be exempt from the confidentiality rule so that placings and scores can be announced. Practice events are all round-robin tournaments; each pair of players will face off with their privately submitted entries and gain a point for each win.

For this practice round, we'll be playing Weighted[100, 10]. What's that? Well now winning slot against your opponent contributes to your in-match score (Slots are 0-indexed). Most in-match points wins the match as usual. Left-most tiebreaker rule has been replaced with right-most tiebreaker rule. Day 1 Practice Round 2 final standings tiebreaker also still applies.

I repeat, you may discuss strategy in thread, and there is no real deadline for submission. Feel free to ask questions.

basically, the circumstances of HF claiming VT come from town 99% of the time. It's a relatively new player coming into a game and immediately have a couple people try to vote them, and super overreacting and claiming as a result. Since scum have daychat scum never, ever does this - there's no group of three scum players in the game dumb enough to think it's necessary and it draws a lot of attention on HF, which is exactly what a newb scum player doesn't want. Meanwhile, overreacting to attacks is the most newbtown behavioral pattern I've seen in AoPS mafia, and doing so to this extreme I have literally never seen come from scum - and what's more, when newb scum do overreact, they virtually always through in a huge helping of OMGUS and start deathtunneling the person who attacked them, which HF did not even begin to do. In addition, pretty much all of the posts immediately surrounding the claim are screaming town overreaction, especially
HistoricFoodie wrote:
Man, I sure do love being deathtunneled.
Quote:
All in all, if you just reread the surrounding posts, it's a response to early pressure that pretty much always comes from town. There are few things HF could post after the ten or so posts either side of his claim that would convince me he is not town. Lastly, from a purely mechanical perspective, scum has no reason to claim VT ever in this setup, unless town wins an investigative role via event and uses it on them, in which case they could try to start a dome. Town shouldn't claim either but town is far more likely to claim for no reason than scum especially with scum having daychat, since scum play more scared than town by definition (at least among noobs).

Announcement

Strange Blotto? Well its D2 Event time.

Suppose you submit a set of non-negative integers and someone else submits a set of non-negative integers under the restriction that , then you would win in-match points. All tiebreaking procedures remain unchanged.

Submissions will remain open until EoD or earlier if everyone submits before then.
post_canonical:
[b]Announcement[/b]

Strange Blotto? Well its D2 Event time.

Suppose you submit a set of non-negative integers $[a_0, a_1, \dots , a_9]$ and someone else submits a set of non-negative integers $[b_0, b_1, \dots , b_9]$ under the restriction that $\sum_{i=0}^{9} a_i = \sum_{i=0}^{9} b_i = 100$, then you would win $\prod_{i=0}^{9} \text{max}(a_i - b_i, 1)$ in-match points. All tiebreaking procedures remain unchanged.

Submissions will remain open until EoD or earlier if everyone submits before then.
